outputs.description: raw shard review content preserved before schema wrapping

accepted_fields_or_changes.cause: The technical seed pool includes one short seed (S_ADD_LARGE_TRANSFER) alongside richer docs, but the note only says to avoid claiming absent fields.
accepted_fields_or_changes.result: Sparse-field cases such as UI/UX or infrastructure can still produce weak overclaims unless the runner has an explicit fallback order.
accepted_fields_or_changes.recommended_change: Add a preferred-seed order or a 'return not found/none' fallback so sparse documents do not get padded with guessed technical fields.

rejected_or_risky_items.cause: The runner note says to run at least one seed and only run both if time allows, but it does not explicitly bind the run to the selected-project memory or forbid cross-seed synthesis.
rejected_or_risky_items.result: A runner could pick the wrong project seed or blend evidence across the two project docs, which would overstate contract-field coverage on sparse rows.
rejected_or_risky_items.recommended_change: Change the note to: choose the seed that matches selected-project memory, use one fixed seed per run, and abstain rather than blend if the needed field is not present.

reviewed_cases.assessment: clean concrete single-doc case; S_BIFF matches target doc and one fixed seed is enough

clean concrete single-doc case; S_REDCROSS_DR matches target doc and one fixed seed is enough
selected-project contract extract is plausible; one fixed seed is enough, but seed choice must be tied to selected-project memory
same selected-project contract pattern as Q038; measurable gate set looks reasonable

participation-limit extraction is measurable via contract fields; one chosen seed should suffice
budget/notice extraction is plausible and single-seed runnable
schedule/period extraction is plausible and single-seed runnable
deliverable extraction is plausible and single-seed runnable
date/window extraction is plausible and single-seed runnable

data/access-control extraction is plausible; metric and gate are measurable
UI/UX extraction may be sparse, but the runner note correctly warns against claiming absent fields
privacy/security extraction is plausible; one technical-rich seed is enough if unsupported fields are left empty

proposal.next_step: Keep Q024/Q026/Q027 as-is, and patch the selected-project notes for Q038-Q045 plus a sparse-field fallback rule for Q046-Q050 before release.
